docs.title: NUH Research Metadata
docs.text:
This site is the data dictionary and metadata reference for research datasets from Nottingham University Hospitals NHS Trust (NUH), one of the largest acute NHS trusts in England, serving a population of approximately 2.5 million across Nottinghamshire and surrounding counties.

The pages here describe each dataset's structure: its fields, data types, permitted values, and coding standards. All datasets are pseudonymised research views; access to the data requires a formal data access request.

docs.title: Systemic Anti-Cancer Therapy (SACT)
docs.text:
2009–present · Quarterly

Patient-level records of systemic anti-cancer treatments (chemotherapy, targeted therapies, immunotherapy, and other drug-based cancer treatments) administered.

What it contains:

Domain Detail Patient Pseudonymised NHS number, year and month of birth, gender, Lower Layer Super Output Area (LLSOA) Diagnosis Primary ICD diagnosis, ICD-O morphology, SNOMED CT diagnosis code Regimen Treatment regimen acronym, intent (curative/palliative/adjuvant), adjunctive therapy type, co-morbidity adjustment, clinical trial flag Cycle Cycle number, start date, weight, WHO performance status Drug administration Drug name (BNF), dm+d code, dose, unit of measurement, route of administration (coded and SNOMED CT), administration date, administering organisation Outcomes Dose reduction, regimen completion (curative), reason not completed, non-curative benefit, toxicity

Coding standards: ICD-10, ICD-O-3, SNOMED CT, NHS dm+d

docs.title: Cancer Outcomes and Services Dataset (COSD)
docs.text:
Full cancer pathway · Nottinghamshire

Patient-level records spanning the cancer care pathway at NUH.

What it contains:

Domain Detail Patient Pseudonymised NHS number and unique record ID, year and month of birth, gender, sexual orientation, ethnicity, GP practice, LLSOA Referral and first seen Source of referral, dates of first outpatient appointment and first cancer specialist review, organisation identifiers Diagnosis Primary ICD-10 diagnosis, ICD-O-3 morphology and topography, SNOMED CT diagnosis code, basis of diagnosis, tumour laterality, grade of differentiation, Ki-67, performance status Non-primary pathway Date of recurrence/relapse, original ICD-10 diagnosis, metastatic type and site (recurrence and progression), method of relapse detection, transformation morphology Staging Pre-treatment and integrated TNM stage (T/N/M categories and overall grouping), TNM edition and version, site-specific staging, staging organisation and date Diagnostic procedures Up to 3 procedures: OPCS-4 and SNOMED CT procedure codes, sentinel node biopsy outcome, organisation, date Pathology Secondary diagnoses, familial cancer syndrome, functional syndrome classification, metastatic type and site at diagnosis Treatment Up to 4 treatment episodes: modality, intent, adjunctive therapy, start and end dates, surgical procedures (OPCS-4 and SNOMED CT), surgeon identifiers, ASA score, surgical access, discharge destination Observations Height, weight, and BMI (up to 2 occasions) Lifestyle Tobacco smoking status and cessation, alcohol history, diabetes, menopausal status, physical activity Holistic needs assessment Assessment offered, care plan status and date, point of pathway, staff role (up to 2 assessments) MDT MDT discussion date, cancer care plan intent, planned treatment types, reason for no treatment; up to 10 MDT meetings with type and date Acute oncology Up to 3 acute oncology assessments: date, location, patient type, outcome Laboratory results LDH, beta-hCG, and AFP values (up to 3 occasions) Imaging Up to 10 imaging episodes: NICIP and SNOMED CT codes, modality, anatomical site, laterality, outcome, report text

Coding standards: ICD-10, ICD-O-3, SNOMED CT, OPCS-4, NICIP

docs.title: Geographic and Temporal Coverage
docs.text: Dataset Geography Start End Frequency SACT Nottinghamshire, England January 2009 Ongoing Quarterly COSD Nottinghamshire, England January 2024 Ongoing TBC

docs.title: NUH Cancer Outcomes and Services Dataset (COSD)
docs.text:
Clinical dataset documenting cancer outcomes and services for patients diagnosed and treated at Nottingham University Hospitals NHS Trust. Covers the full cancer pathway from referral through diagnosis, staging, treatment, and follow-up, including both primary and non-primary (recurrence/relapse) pathways.

URI: https://health-informatics-uon.github.io/nuh-data/datasets/cosd

Name: cosd

docs.title: Subsets
docs.text: Subset Description

docs.title: Enum: AdjunctiveTherapy
docs.text:
Adjunctive therapy is therapy given in addition to the main therapy to maximize its effectiveness.

This field allows for the accurate recording of these to determine if Adjunctive therapy was Adjuvant (After the main therapy) or Neo-Adjuvant (Before the main therapy) or not applicable.

docs.title: Enum: AnatomicalSideImaging
docs.text: The side of the body that is the subject of an Imaging or Radiodiagnostic Event.

docs.title: Enum: AsaScore
docs.text: The ASA physical status classification system is a system for assessing the fitness of patients before surgery.

docs.title: Enum: AssessmentCarePlanPointOfPathway
docs.text: The point of the pathway where a Holistic Needs Assessment or Personalised Care and Support Plan is offered, declined, completed, not required, or unable to be completed

docs.title: Enum: AssessmentCarePlanStatus
docs.text: An indication of whether a PATIENT has completed a Holistic Needs Assessment (HNA) and /or Personalised Care and Support Plan (PCSP) was offered/completed

docs.title: Enum: AssessmentLocation
docs.text: The location where the Acute Oncology (AO) assessment was performed within the health care provider

docs.title: Enum: AssessmentOffered
docs.text: An indication of whether a PATIENT has been offered a Holistic Needs Assessment (HNA)

docs.title: Enum: BasisOfDiagnosisCancer
docs.text: This is the method used to confirm the cancer.

docs.title: Class: COSDResearchView
docs.text: Flat research view combining file metadata, patient demographics, primary pathway, non-primary pathway, diagnostic procedures, staging, treatment, and outcomes data. Corresponds to RVW.COSD_RESEARCH_VIEW in the source database. Each row represents one COSD record.

docs.title: Enum: CancerCarePlanIntent
docs.text: The intention of a Cancer Care Plan developed within a Cancer Care Spell.

docs.title: Enum: CancerImagingModality
docs.text: The type of imaging procedure used during an Imaging or Radiodiagnostic Event for a Cancer Care Spell. NB: PET Scan also includes PET-CT Scan.

docs.title: Enum: CancerTreatmentIntent
docs.text:
The intention of the cancer treatment provided during a Cancer Care Spell.

* Disease Modification is Drug Specific ** Diagnostic and Staging are Surgery Specific

docs.title: Enum: CancerTreatmentModalityRegistration
docs.text:
The type of treatment or care which was delivered in a Cancer Treatment Period.

This is subset of Cancer Treatment Modality, which supports Cancer Registration in England

docs.title: Enum: ClinicalNurseSpecialistIndicationCode
docs.text: Record if and when the patient saw an appropriate site specific clinical nurse specialist.

docs.title: Curie
docs.text:
search: boost: 1.0 ---# Type: Curie

a compact URI

docs.title: Date
docs.text:
search: boost: 1.0 ---# Type: Date

a date (year, month and day) in an idealized calendar

docs.title: DateOrDatetime
docs.text:
search: boost: 1.0 ---# Type: DateOrDatetime

Either a date or a datetime

docs.title: Datetime
docs.text:
search: boost: 1.0 ---# Type: Datetime

The combination of a date and time

docs.title: Decimal
docs.text:
search: boost: 1.0 ---# Type: Decimal

A real number with arbitrary precision that conforms to the xsd:decimal specification

docs.title: Enum: DestinationOfDischargeHospitalProviderSpell
docs.text: This records the destination of a PATIENT on completion of the Hospital Provider Spell. It can also indicate that the PATIENT died.

docs.title: Enum: DiabetesMellitusType1AndType2Indicator
docs.text: Does the patient have a diagnosis of type 1 or type 2 diabetes mellitus?

docs.title: Double
docs.text:
search: boost: 1.0 ---# Type: Double

A real number that conforms to the xsd:double specification

docs.title: Enum: EthnicCategory
docs.text:
The ethnicity of a PERSON, as specified by the PERSON. The 16+1 ethnic data categories defined in the 2001 census is the national mandatory standard for the collection and analysis of ethnicity.

(The Office for National Statistics has developed a further breakdown of the group from that given, which may be used locally.)

docs.title: Enum: FamilialCancerSyndrome
docs.text: Indicate whether there is a possible or confirmed familial cancer syndrome

docs.title: Float
docs.text:
search: boost: 1.0 ---# Type: Float

A real number that conforms to the xsd:float specification

docs.title: Enum: FunctionalSyndromeClassificationIndicator
docs.text: Indicate whether there is a possible or confirmed Functional syndrome classification

docs.title: Enum: FunctionalSyndromeClassificationType
docs.text:
Specify the type of functional syndrome classification the patient is diagnosed with.

For Neuroendocrine tumours (NET) only.

docs.title: Enum: GradeOfDifferentiationAtDiagnosis
docs.text: GRADE OF DIFFERENTIATION (AT DIAGNOSIS) is the definitive grade of the Tumour at the time of PATIENT DIAGNOSIS.

docs.title: Enum: HistoryOfAlcoholCurrent
docs.text:
Specify the current history of alcohol consumption for the patient (≤3 months) from date of diagnosis

These are based on the UK Chief Medical Officers' Alcohol Guideline Review (Jan 2016)

docs.title: Enum: HistoryOfAlcoholPast
docs.text:
Specify the past history of alcohol consumption for the patient (>3 months) from date of diagnosis

These are based on the UK Chief Medical Officers' Alcohol Guideline Review (Jan 2016)

docs.title: Enum: ImagingOutcome
docs.text: Record the outcome for the imaging event as agreed with the radiologist or clinical team

docs.title: Integer
docs.text:
search: boost: 1.0 ---# Type: Integer

An integer

docs.title: Jsonpath
docs.text:
search: boost: 1.0 ---# Type: Jsonpath

A string encoding a JSON Path. The value of the string MUST conform to JSON Point syntax and SHOULD dereference to zero or more valid objects within the current instance document when encoded in tree form.

docs.title: Jsonpointer
docs.text:
search: boost: 1.0 ---# Type: Jsonpointer

A string encoding a JSON Pointer. The value of the string MUST conform to JSON Point syntax and SHOULD dereference to a valid object within the current instance document when encoded in tree form.

docs.title: Enum: MenopausalStatus
docs.text: Record the Menopausal Status (at the point of diagnosis) of female patients only

docs.title: Enum: MetastaticSite
docs.text:
The site of the metastatic disease, if any

More than one site can be recorded

docs.title: Enum: MetastaticType
docs.text: Indicate the type of metastatic disease diagnosed by the clinical team

docs.title: Enum: MultidisciplinaryTeamMeetingDiscussion
docs.text: Record if the patient was not discussed within a MDT Meeting

docs.title: Enum: MultidisciplinaryTeamMeetingDiscussionType
docs.text: Record if the patient was discussed within a Multidisciplinary Team Meeting (MDTM)

docs.title: Enum: MultidisciplinaryTeamMeetingType
docs.text:
Record the type of MDT meeting at which the patient was discussed. Please provide the most detailed level of information that is possible.

Note: the codes at the high level (shown in bold) are Tumour groups and the items below each high-level code are Multidisciplinary Teams. ORGANISATIONS should only use the high-level code if the Multidisciplinary Team is not listed.

docs.title: Ncname
docs.text:
search: boost: 1.0 ---# Type: Ncname

Prefix part of CURIE

docs.title: Enum: NhsNumberStatusIndicatorCode
docs.text: The NHS NUMBER STATUS INDICATOR CODE is the trace status of the NHS NUMBER

docs.title: Enum: NoCancerTreatmentReason
docs.text: The main reason why no active cancer treatment is specified within a Cancer Care Plan.

docs.title: Nodeidentifier
docs.text:
search: boost: 1.0 ---# Type: Nodeidentifier

A URI, CURIE or BNODE that represents a node in a model.

docs.title: Objectidentifier
docs.text:
search: boost: 1.0 ---# Type: Objectidentifier

A URI or CURIE that represents an object in the model.

docs.title: Enum: Outcome
docs.text: Record the outcome of the acute oncology episode

docs.title: Enum: PalliativeCareSpecialistSeenIndicatorCancerRecurrence
docs.text: Record whether the patient was seen by a palliative care specialist. This would be a member of the specialist palliative care team led by a consultant in palliative medicine for a recurrence of cancer.

docs.title: Enum: PatientType
docs.text:
Record the type each patient presentation is grouped within, as a result of the acute oncology assessment.

A table is available within the user guide to provide additional information.

docs.title: Enum: PerformanceStatusAdult
docs.text: A World Health Organisation classification indicating a PERSON's status relating to activity / disability.

docs.title: Enum: PersonSexualOrientationCodeAtDiagnosis
docs.text:
Person's sexual orientation as self-declared at the time of the PATIENT DIAGNOSIS.

This complies with the information standard DCB2094.

docs.title: Enum: PersonStatedGenderCode
docs.text: Person's gender as self-declared (or inferred by observation for those unable to declare their PERSON STATED GENDER).

docs.title: Enum: PhysicalActivityCurrent
docs.text: Specify the current physical activity level

docs.title: Enum: PlannedCancerTreatmentType
docs.text: This is the clinically proposed treatment, usually agreed at a Multi Disciplinary Team Meeting, and may not be the same as the treatment which is subsequently agreed with the patient. More than one planned treatment type may be recorded and these may either be alternative or sequential treatments.

docs.title: Enum: ProfessionalRegistrationIssuerCodeConsultantFirstSeen

docs.title: cosd
docs.text:
Clinical dataset documenting cancer outcomes and services for patients diagnosed and treated at Nottingham University Hospitals NHS Trust. Covers the full cancer pathway from referral through diagnosis, staging, treatment, and follow-up, including both primary and non-primary (recurrence/relapse) pathways.

URI: https://health-informatics-uon.github.io/nuh-data/datasets/cosd

docs.title: Slot: cr6540_treat1_adjunctive_therapy
docs.text:
Adjunctive therapy is therapy given in addition to the main therapy to maximize its effectiveness.

This field allows for the accurate recording of these to determine if Adjunctive therapy was Adjuvant (After the main therapy) or Neo-Adjuvant (Before the main therapy) or not applicable.

docs.text: This is the method used to confirm the cancer.

docs.title: Slot: pp_cr0410_grade_of_differentiationat_diag
docs.text: GRADE OF DIFFERENTIATION (AT DIAGNOSIS) is the definitive grade of the Tumour at the time of PATIENT DIAGNOSIS.

docs.title: Slot: pp_cr0480_topography_icd_o_3
docs.text: The topographical site code for the tumour as defined by ICD-O-3. This will normally be derived by Registries.

docs.title: Slot: pp_cr0510_performance_status_adult
docs.text: A World Health Organisation classification indicating a PERSON's status relating to activity / disability.

docs.title: Slot: pp_cr0520_t_category_final_pretreatment
docs.text: T CATEGORY (FINAL PRETREATMENT) is the code which classifies the size and extent of the primary tumour before treatment.

docs.title: Slot: pp_cr0540_n_category_final_pretreatment
docs.text: N CATEGORY (FINAL PRETREATMENT) is the code which classifies the absence or presence and extent of regional lymph node metastases before treatment.

docs.title: Slot: pp_cr0560_m_category_final_pretreatment
docs.text: M CATEGORY (FINAL PRETREATMENT) is the code which classifies the absence or presence of distant metastases pre treatment.

docs.title: Slot: pp_cr0580_tnm_stage_grouping_final_pretreatment
docs.text:
Record the overall clinical TNM stage grouping of the tumour, derived from each T, N and M component prior to treatment. This classification is based on all the evidence available to the clinician(s) with responsibility for assessing the patient and for the patient’s treatment plan. Such evidence arises from physical examination, imaging, endoscopy, biopsy, surgical exploration and other relevant examinations.

The overall pretreatment TNM stage grouping indicates the tumour stage at the time the treatment plan was devised.

docs.title: Slot: pp_cr0610_tnm_stage_grouping_integrated
docs.text:
Record the overall TNM stage grouping of the tumour, derived from each T, N and M component after treatment. This classification is based on all the evidence available to the clinician(s) with responsibility for assessing the patient. Such evidence arises from physical examination, imaging, endoscopy, biopsy, surgical exploration and other relevant examinations.

The overall integrated TNM stage grouping indicates the tumour stage after treatment and/or after all available evidence has been collected.

docs.title: Slot: pp_cr0620_t_category_integrated_stage
docs.text: T CATEGORY (INTEGRATED) is the code which classifies the size and extent of the primary tumour after treatment and/or after all available evidence has been collected.

docs.title: Slot: pp_cr0630_n_category_integrated_stage
docs.text: N CATEGORY (INTEGRATED) is the code which classifies the absence or presence and extent of regional lymph node metastases after treatment and/or after all available evidence has been collected.

docs.title: Slot: pp_cr0640_m_category_integrated_stage
docs.text: M CATEGORY (INTEGRATED) is the code classifies the absence or presence of distant metastases after treatment and/or after all available evidence has been collected.

docs.title: Slot: pp_cr1360_date_first_seen_cancer_specialist

docs.title: Slot: pp_cr7600_primary_diag_subsidiary_comment
docs.text: Additional comments on diagnosis where coding is difficult or imprecise. (Examples of this would be: "papillary glioneuronal tumour" or "angiocentric glioma" to specify recently described diagnoses which do not have ICD10 or ICD-O-3 coding. "anaplastic ependymoma" or "ependymoblastoma" to distinguish between these two diagnoses which may have different treatment decisions or outcomes but which cannot be distinguished in ICD10 or ICD-O-3 coding.)

docs.title: Slot: pp_cr7610_secondary_diag_icd1

docs.title: Slot: pp_cr7620_other_significant_diag_subsidiary_comment

(For example "Li-Fraumeni", "Rhabdoid tumour predisposition syndrome" or "Biallelic PMS2 mutation" to identify distinct syndromes which may have different treatment decisions or outcomes but cannot be coded separately.)

docs.title: NUH Systemic Anti-Cancer Therapy Dataset (SACT)
docs.text:
Clinical dataset documenting systemic anti-cancer treatment records from Nottingham University Hospitals NHS Trust. Covers patients receiving systemic anti-cancer therapies (chemotherapy and other systemic treatments) from 2009 onwards, capturing patient demographics, regimen details, cycle data, and individual drug administrations.

URI: https://health-informatics-uon.github.io/nuh-data/datasets/sact

Name: sact

docs.title: Class: SACTResearchView
docs.text: Flat research view combining patient, regimen, cycle, and drug administration data. Corresponds to RVW.SACT_RESEARCH_VIEW in the source database. Each row represents a single drug administration event, with regimen and patient context repeated per row.

docs.title: Induced

  a single drug administration event, with regimen and patient context repeated per
  row.

docs.title: Enum: YesNoIndicator
docs.text: Simple yes/no indicator.

docs.title: Slot: actual_dose_per_admin
docs.text: Dose in mg or other applicable unit for each administration in a SACT cycle.

docs.title: Slot: adjunctive_therapy
docs.text: Adjunctive therapy is therapy given in addition to the main therapy to maximise its effectiveness. Records whether the therapy was Adjuvant (after the main therapy), Neo-Adjuvant (before the main therapy), or not applicable.

docs.title: Slot: admin_meas_per_actual_dose
docs.text: Units of measurement used for each administration in a SACT cycle.

docs.title: Slot: administration_date
docs.text: The date on which the anti-cancer drug was administered to a patient, an infusion commenced, or an oral drug initially dispensed to the patient.

docs.title: Slot: clinical_trial
docs.text: Records whether the patient is currently in an active Systemic Anti-Cancer Therapy trial.

docs.title: Slot: co_morbidity_adjustment
docs.text: Whether the patient's overall physical state (other diseases and conditions) was a significant factor in deciding on the regimen, or in varying the dose or treatment interval.

docs.title: Slot: consultant_gmc_code
docs.text: Code of the consultant who initiated the SACT treatment. Derived from the GENERAL MEDICAL COUNCIL REFERENCE NUMBER.

docs.title: Slot: consultant_spec_code
docs.text: Specialty code of the consultant who initiated the SACT treatment.

docs.title: Slot: cycle_number
docs.text: Cycle number, numbered sequentially within each regimen.

docs.title: Slot: date_decision_to_treat
docs.text: The date on which the consultation between the PATIENT and the clinician took place and a Planned Cancer Treatment was agreed.

docs.title: Slot: date_of_birth_mid_month
docs.text: Approximate date of birth using the 15th of the birth month, for use in age calculations while preserving patient privacy.

docs.title: Slot: date_of_birth_month
docs.text: Month component of the patient's date of birth, extracted from DATE OF BIRTH for privacy preservation.

docs.title: Slot: date_of_birth_year
docs.text: Year component of the patient's date of birth, extracted from DATE OF BIRTH for privacy preservation.

docs.title: Slot: diagnosis_code_snomed
docs.text: SNOMED CT concept ID identifying the clinical diagnosis given to the patient.

docs.title: Slot: dm_d
docs.text: The unique ID from the dm+d (Dictionary of Medicines and Devices) database, based around the drug name and manufacturer. Pilot item for v3.0, required only for official Pilot sites.

docs.title: Slot: drug_name
docs.text: The name of the anti-cancer drug, taken from the British National Formulary.

docs.title: Slot: height_start_reg
docs.text: Height in metres at start of SACT regimen.

docs.title: Slot: intent_of_treatment
docs.text: Intent of the SACT regimen.

docs.title: Slot: llsoa
docs.text: Lower Layer Super Output Area, derived from PATIENT POSTCODE for geographic analysis while preserving patient privacy.

docs.title: Slot: morphology_icd_o
docs.text: Morphology coded using ICD-O at time of decision to treat. This is the Morphology ICD-O at the start of the Systemic Anti-Cancer Therapy. Required for Haematology cases.

docs.title: Slot: nhs_number_status
docs.text: The NHS NUMBER STATUS INDICATOR CODE indicates the verification status of the NHS number provided.

docs.title: Slot: org_id_code_provider
docs.text: The ORGANISATION IDENTIFIER of the Organisation acting as a Health Care Provider. Complies with ANANA standard DCB0090.

docs.title: Slot: org_identifier_sact_admin
docs.text: Organisation Identifier of the organisation responsible for each administration in a SACT cycle. Complies with ANANA standard DCB0090.

docs.title: Slot: ot_admin_meas_per_actual_dose
docs.text: Free-text description of a unit of measurement not available within the coded [admin_meas_per_actual_dose] field. Only populated when code 98 (Other) is selected.

docs.title: Slot: perf_stat_start_cycle_ad
docs.text: WHO performance status at start of the cycle, for patients aged 19 years and above.

docs.title: Slot: perf_status_start_reg_ad
docs.text: WHO performance status at start of the regimen, for patients aged 19 years and above.

docs.title: Slot: person_stated_gender_code
docs.text: Person's gender as self-declared (or inferred by observation for those unable to declare their PERSON STATED GENDER).

docs.title: Slot: primary_diagnosis
docs.text: Primary diagnosis at time of decision to treat, coded using ICD. This is the PRIMARY DIAGNOSIS (ICD) at the start of the Systemic Anti-Cancer Therapy.

docs.title: Slot: pseudo_nhs_number
docs.text: The NHS NUMBER, the primary identifier of a PERSON, is a unique identifier for a PATIENT within the NHS in England and Wales. Pseudonymised for research use.

docs.title: Slot: regimen
docs.text: The acronym derived from the drugs used in the Anti-Cancer Drug Regimen, used to identify the drugs used in the regimen.

docs.title: Slot: route_admin_snomed_ct_dm_d
docs.text: The SNOMED CT concept ID from the NHS Dictionary of Medicines and Devices identifying the route of administration.

docs.title: sact
docs.text:
Clinical dataset documenting systemic anti-cancer treatment records from Nottingham University Hospitals NHS Trust. Covers patients receiving systemic anti-cancer therapies (chemotherapy and other systemic treatments) from 2009 onwards, capturing patient demographics, regimen details, cycle data, and individual drug administrations.

URI: https://health-informatics-uon.github.io/nuh-data/datasets/sact

docs.title: Slot: sact_administration_route
docs.text: The prescribed method of delivery for each administration in a SACT cycle.

docs.title: Slot: start_date_cycle
docs.text: Date of first drug administration in each cycle.

docs.title: Slot: start_date_regimen
docs.text: The first administration date of the first cycle of a regimen.

docs.title: Slot: unit_meas_snomed_ct_dm_d
docs.text: The SNOMED CT concept ID from the NHS Dictionary of Medicines and Devices identifying the unit of measurement.

docs.title: Slot: weight_start_cycle
docs.text: Weight in kilogrammes at start of the cycle.

docs.title: Slot: weight_start_of_reg
docs.text: Weight in kilogrammes at start of SACT regimen.
